headline: Turkish bank funding season and a steady 37% CBRT hold dominate the desk-relevant flow, with rating momentum still constructive.
summary: The most recent verifiable items point to an active Turkish bank term-funding window, led by İş Bankası's roughly $1.3bn sustainable syndicated loan, against a backdrop of the CBRT holding its policy rate at 37%. Rating tone remains supportive after Fitch improved Türkiye's outlook while Moody's held, following Moody's mid-2025 upgrade to three notches below investment grade. Primary-market appetite has been evident since Türkiye opened the year's EM dollar issuance, and banks have been rolling syndicated facilities at high rollover rates, longer tenors and lower costs. Note: fresh 24-hour wire flow could not be confirmed from available sources, so only verified recent items are included below.

items.title: İş Bankası Secures Nearly $1.3 Billion in Sustainable Syndicated Loan
items.summary: İş Bankası closed a sustainability-linked syndicated loan of nearly $1.3 billion, extending the Turkish bank term-funding season.
items.angle: Live DCM/financing lead: participation, ESG-tranche structuring and associated cross-currency/rate hedging on the FX-denominated tranche, plus a read-across to other Turkish banks' upcoming rollovers.

items.title: Turkey's central bank keeps policy rate unchanged at 37 percent
items.summary: The CBRT held its one-week repo policy rate at 37%, keeping its cautious stance amid the disinflation path.
items.angle: Rates positioning and hedging signal: informs TLREF/receiver structures, carry trades and forward pricing for clients ahead of the next MPC decision.

items.title: Turkish banks conclude syndicated loan renewals with high rollover rates, longer maturities and lower costs
items.summary: Turkish banks completed syndicated loan renewals achieving high rollover ratios, longer tenors and reduced spreads, reflecting improved external funding conditions.
items.angle: Competitor/mandate tracking and financing lead: benchmark for pricing new facilities and identifying which Turkish FIs still need to refinance and hedge FX funding.

items.title: Fitch Boosts Turkey's Rating Outlook, Moody's Stays Put
items.summary: Fitch improved Türkiye's sovereign rating outlook while Moody's held its rating steady, underscoring diverging but broadly constructive agency views.
items.angle: Credit/market impact: supportive for sovereign and bank CDS and Eurobond spreads, aiding primary issuance windows and credit hedging discussions with clients.

items.title: Turkey Joins EM Bond Rush With Year's First Dollar Note Issuance
items.summary: Türkiye opened the year's EM primary calendar with its first dollar bond sale, capitalising on strong early-year demand.
items.angle: DCM lead and issuance-window read: gauges investor appetite and pricing for follow-on sovereign, bank and corporate Eurobond mandates.

items.title: Moody's upgrades Turkey to three notches below investment grade
items.summary: Moody's upgraded Türkiye's sovereign rating to three notches below investment grade, continuing the multi-agency improvement trend.
items.angle: Credit backdrop for positioning: tighter sovereign risk premia support banks' and corporates' external borrowing costs and inform relative-value and CDS trades.
